what_it_does: Defines a wave-driven interpretive pass that dispatches one Opus agent per batch to read original files and emit neutral, file-local interpretive JSON outputs for a ledger of code/design substrates.
observations: Exports a meta object describing the script's purpose and phase.
Hardcodes WAVE and N constants that must be edited per wave.
Generates zero-padded batch ids from 1 to N.
Builds a per-batch prompt instructing an agent to read a JSON allocation file and process each entry atomically.
The prompt mandates a specific set of output JSON keys and prohibits ranking or cross-file claims.
Uses parallel(...) to run N agents and returns a summary object.
standouts: The prompt itself is the main artifact; it encodes a strict ontology of interpretive fields.
Atomic one-file-in/one-file-out rule with neutral, contribution-not-ranking framing.
Agent dispatch uses model 'opus' and phase 'Interpret'.
conventions: await parallel(...) pattern for concurrent agent execution.
concerns: WAVE and N are manually edited constants, a per-run maintenance point.
No validation of batch file existence or output directory creation in the script itself; delegated to the agent.
notes: This is the orchestrator prompt/script for a single interpretive wave; the real specification lives inside the prompt string.
questions: What does the parallel(...) helper provide, and is it a global runtime function?
How are agent failures handled beyond res.filter(Boolean)?
Is the meta object consumed by a scheduler or only for documentation?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to add a neutral interpretive layer per file via Opus agents; the script does exactly that by generating prompts and dispatching agents, though it leaves file I/O to the agents.
intention_signals: It tries to scale a careful, human-like interpretive reading across many files by giving each Opus agent a narrow, self-contained batch and a strict output schema, so later fusion can treat every file's interpretation as an equal contribution.
novelty: The interpretive prompt defines a rich per-file ontology (what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, intention_for_embedding) rather than a simple summary.
contribution: Provides a reusable, wave-parameterized orchestration pattern and a detailed prompt schema for generating neutral, file-local interpretive JSONs that can feed a fused ledger without ranking files against each other.
summary_for_embedding: A JavaScript orchestrator that dispatches Opus agents in parallel batches to read original files and write neutral interpretive JSONs according to a fixed schema.
intention_for_embedding: To automate a neutral, file-local interpretive layer for a code/design ledger by running one Opus agent per batch.

symbols.does: Exports metadata describing the script's name, description, and interpretive phase.
symbols.description: The meta object names the ledger-interpret-wave pass, describes its neutral file-local purpose, and declares a single 'Interpret' phase, serving as a manifest for the orchestrator.

symbols.does: Holds the wave directory path that must be edited for each run.
symbols.description: A string constant set to 'build-prep/the-one-system/interpret/wave-005'; it parameterizes where batch allocation files and outputs live, requiring manual update per wave.

symbols.does: Defines the number of batches/agents for the current wave.
symbols.description: An integer constant set to 40; it controls how many parallel interpretive agents are dispatched and how many batch ids are generated.

symbols.does: Generates an array of zero-padded batch identifier strings from 1 to N.
symbols.description: Created with Array.from, this array produces strings like '001', '002', ..., '040', which are used to locate per-batch allocation files and label agent tasks.

symbols.does: Returns the full prompt string for a given batch id.
symbols.description: A template function that builds a detailed instruction for an Opus agent: read the batch allocation JSON, read each original file, and write a neutral interpretive JSON with a fixed set of fields, while forbidding ranking and cross-file claims.

symbols.does: Collects results from parallel agent invocations.
symbols.description: Awaited call to parallel(...) maps each batch id to an agent(prompt(id), ...) task using model 'opus' and phase 'Interpret', then filters truthy results to count completed agents.
